highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 upgrades to Skia m151 and ships a wave of managed-code performance optimizations.
highlights_body: Multiple hot-path operations — SKColor conversions, HarfBuzz buffer encoding, SKShaper glyph reading, and hex parsing — are ported to allocation-free managed code. WASM support expands to Emscripten 5.0.6 and .NET 11 with new exception-handling support.

bullets.detail: The bundled Skia engine is now built from chrome/m151, bringing upstream rendering bug fixes and performance improvements across all platforms.
SK_ENABLE_LEGACY_SHADERCONTEXT is re-enabled to fix a CPU raster rendering regression introduced in an upstream sync.

bullets.lead: SKColor and SKColorF conversions ported to managed code
bullets.detail: SKColor-to-SKColorF conversion and SKPMColor single-value premultiply/unpremultiply are now pure managed integer math, eliminating P/Invoke round-trips on every conversion.

bullets.lead: SKColor hex parsing optimized with ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads
bullets.detail: The hex color parsing path is rewritten for zero-allocation span-based input, and new ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads are added for consumers who already have sliced strings.
The unnecessary unsafe modifier is removed from GRContextOptions, making the type usable in safe contexts without opting in to unsafe.

bullets.lead: Native resource leak in SKImage.FromEncodedData fixed
bullets.detail: SKImage.FromEncodedData(SKData, SKRectI) was leaking a native image handle when the subset crop failed; the handle is now correctly released.
HarfBuzzSharp.Buffer.AddUtf8(string) now uses a pooled UTF-8 encode path instead of allocating a byte array on every call, cutting allocations in text shaping loops.

bullets.lead: Tag.Parse and SKFourByteTag.Parse no longer allocate per call
bullets.detail: HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse and SKFourByteTag.Parse each previously allocated a char[4] on every invocation; both now use stack-allocated spans.

bullets.lead: SKShaper glyph results read via zero-copy spans
bullets.detail: SKShaper now reads glyph and position arrays from native memory via ReadOnlySpan without copying into managed arrays, reducing allocations in the shaping hot path.

bullets.lead: WASM updated to Emscripten 5.0.6 with .NET 11 exception handling
bullets.detail: WebAssembly native libraries are rebuilt with Emscripten 5.0.6, enabling the new exnref-based exception-handling model required by .NET 11 WASM. A preview-.NET WASM CI job is added to catch exception-handling regressions early.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Emscripten 5.0.6 WASM static libraries, Skia submodule WASM build fix, and golden-image visual regression test harness

preview_summaries.v4.151.0-rc.1.1: Release Candidate 1 landed the Skia m151 upstream sync, the CPU raster regression fix, the SKImage resource leak fix, WASM Emscripten 5.0.6 support, and the SKColor/SKPMColor managed conversion ports.
preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.2.1: Preview 2 delivered the HarfBuzz allocation-free AddUtf8 path, Tag.Parse and SKFourByteTag.Parse stack-span rewrites, zero-copy SKShaper glyph spans, SKColor hex parsing optimizations, and the GRContextOptions cleanup.
preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.1.1: Preview 1 opened the 4.151 line with the initial Skia m151 milestone upgrade and a Skia submodule WASM build fix.

harfbuzz_summary: HarfBuzzSharp 14.2.1 ships with this release, updated with allocation-free UTF-8 buffer encoding and stack-allocated tag parsing from the performance work in this cycle.
